channel.title: Chris Ward | MP for Brighton Kemptown and Peacehaven

image.title: Chris Ward | MP for Brighton Kemptown and Peacehaven

item.title: Chris Ward MP welcomes landmark Renters’ Rights Act coming into force
item.dc:creator: Chris Ward
item.description:
Chris Ward MP has welcomed the introduction of the Renters’ Rights Act 2025, which comes into force on 1 May, delivering stronger protections and greater security for millions of renters across England. The new law represents the most significant reform to the private rented sector in decades, introducing a range of measures designed to rebalance...

The post Chris Ward MP welcomes landmark Renters’ Rights Act coming into force appeared first on Chris Ward | MP for Brighton Kemptown and Peacehaven.
item.content:encoded:
Chris Ward MP has welcomed the introduction of the Renters’ Rights Act 2025, which comes into force on 1 May, delivering stronger protections and greater security for millions of renters across England.

The new law represents the most significant reform to the private rented sector in decades, introducing a range of measures designed to rebalance the relationship between tenants and landlords. These include the abolition of Section 21 “no-fault” evictions, an end to fixed-term tenancies in favour of more secure rolling contracts, and new limits on rent increases to once per year.

The Act will also clamp down on unfair practices, including banning rental bidding wars, restricting large upfront rent demands, and preventing discrimination against renters with children or those in receipt of benefits.

Chris Ward MP said:

“I am delighted to see the Renters’ Rights Act come into force. This is a landmark moment for millions of renters who have for too long faced insecurity, unfair treatment, and the constant fear of losing their home through no fault of their own.

“In my maiden speech in Parliament, I highlighted just how important this issue is for so many of my constituents. Too many people in our community have been living with short-term tenancies, rising rents, and very little protection.

“These reforms will give renters greater stability, stronger rights, and the confidence that their home is truly their home. Ending no-fault evictions and introducing more secure tenancies will make a real difference to families, young people, and older renters alike.

“There is more to do to fix the housing crisis, but this is a major step forward in creating a fairer, more secure private rented sector.”

The changes will apply to most private rented tenancies from 1 May, with existing tenancies automatically transitioning to the new system of rolling agreements.

Chris Ward MP reaffirmed his commitment to continuing to work in Government and with local stakeholders to ensure the reforms are effectively implemented and deliver meaningful improvements for renters across Brighton Kemptown and Peacehaven.

NOTES

The Renters’ Right Act Information Sheet 2026 is available here

ENDS

The post Chris Ward MP welcomes landmark Renters’ Rights Act coming into force appeared first on Chris Ward | MP for Brighton Kemptown and Peacehaven.

item.title: Chris Ward MP supports Bowel Cancer Awareness Month
item.dc:creator: Chris Ward
item.description:
Chris Ward MP for Brighton Kemptown and Peacehaven is backing Bowel Cancer Awareness Month this April, highlighting the importance of early diagnosis. Bowel cancer is the UK’s fourth most common cancer and second biggest cancer killer. Around 17,400 people lose their lives to the disease each year. Screening is a powerful tool in helping to...

The post Chris Ward MP supports Bowel Cancer Awareness Month appeared first on Chris Ward | MP for Brighton Kemptown and Peacehaven.
item.content:encoded:
Chris Ward MP for Brighton Kemptown and Peacehaven is backing Bowel Cancer Awareness Month this April, highlighting the importance of early diagnosis.

Bowel cancer is the UK’s fourth most common cancer and second biggest cancer killer. Around 17,400 people lose their lives to the disease each year.

Screening is a powerful tool in helping to diagnose bowel cancer early when the disease is most treatable. It can spot the disease before symptoms start and can even help prevent cancer developing in the first place by identifying pre-cancerous polyps. When diagnosed early, nine in ten people survive bowel cancer.

That’s why this #BowelCancerAwarenessMonth, Bowel Cancer UK are asking everyone to help them raise awareness of the disease, so everyone has the best chance of an early diagnosis.

According to data provided by Cancer Research UK, the Brighton Kemptown and Peacehaven bowel screening uptake stands at 67.1%. There is still work to be done to improve uptake, which is why Mr Ward is keen to encourage participation in the bowel cancer screening programme over the remainder of Bowel Cancer Awareness Month.

Further information on spotting the symptoms of bowel cancer is available here: https://www.bowelcanceruk.org.uk/about-bowel-cancer/symptoms/ .

Commenting, Chris Ward MP said:

“Bowel Cancer Awareness Month is a vital opportunity to raise awareness and improve early diagnosis of the UK’s second biggest cancer killer.

“Screening saves lives, so I strongly encourage everyone over 60 to complete the test when it arrives in the post. And if you’re experiencing any symptoms, please contact your GP without delay.

“Here in Brighton Kemptown and Peacehaven, I am committed to increasing uptake of bowel cancer screening so that more cases are caught early, when treatment is most effective.

“Looking ahead, the new Sussex Cancer Centre at Royal Sussex County Hospital will transform cancer care in our region. With one of the largest cancer populations in the country, it’s right that Sussex has been chosen to deliver faster, more advanced and more specialised treatment for patients.”

Notes

Further information is available at Bowel Cancer UK.

Data is available here.

The post Chris Ward MP supports Bowel Cancer Awareness Month appeared first on Chris Ward | MP for Brighton Kemptown and Peacehaven.

item.title: Chris Ward MP welcomes progress from Royal Mail to improve deliveries across the constituency
item.dc:creator: Chris Ward
item.description:
On Wednesday 8 April, Chris Ward MP for Brighton Kemptown and Peacehaven met with Royal Mail management at his constituency office, following ongoing concerns about delayed deliveries across the area. Mr Ward requested the meeting after writing to Royal Mail demanding a clear plan to address the issues affecting local residents. This follows a sustained...

The post Chris Ward MP welcomes progress from Royal Mail to improve deliveries across the constituency appeared first on Chris Ward | MP for Brighton Kemptown and Peacehaven.
item.content:encoded:
On Wednesday 8 April, Chris Ward MP for Brighton Kemptown and Peacehaven met with Royal Mail management at his constituency office, following ongoing concerns about delayed deliveries across the area.

Mr Ward requested the meeting after writing to Royal Mail demanding a clear plan to address the issues affecting local residents.

This follows a sustained campaign by Mr Ward, who has been encouraging constituents to share their experiences of delays. The responses have helped build a detailed picture of the problem and strengthen calls for urgent improvements.

At the meeting, Royal Mail representatives apologised for the disruption faced by residents and outlined steps being taken to improve performance, including a recruitment drive to increase staffing levels and restore reliable deliveries.

Commenting, Chris Ward MP said:

“Royal Mail is one of our great national institutions and I am determined to see it succeed.

“It has been incredibly frustrating to hear from so many residents, particularly in Saltdean and Rottingdean, about ongoing delivery problems. That’s why I invited Royal Mail to my office to discuss how they are going to better support my constituents.

“This was a constructive meeting and a step in the right direction. Royal Mail recognises the impact this has had on people locally and I want to thank everyone who got in touch with me to share their experiences.

“I have been assured that a recruitment plan is now in place to significantly boost staffing levels in Saltdean and Rottingdean and get deliveries back on track. That’s good news for our local community, and I’ll be monitoring progress closely to make sure residents see the improvements they deserve.”

ENDS

The post Chris Ward MP welcomes progress from Royal Mail to improve deliveries across the constituency appeared first on Chris Ward | MP for Brighton Kemptown and Peacehaven.

item.title: Chris Ward MP champions biggest single funding allocation to deliver more places for children with SEND
item.dc:creator: Chris Ward
item.description:
Chris Ward MP for Brighton Kemptown and Peacehaven has welcomed £8.1 million in funding for East Sussex County Council, as part of the Labour government’s plan to fix the SEND system and end the postcode lottery in support for children and young people. The investment, part of more than £3 billion to create inclusive classrooms...

The post Chris Ward MP champions biggest single funding allocation to deliver more places for children with SEND appeared first on Chris Ward | MP for Brighton Kemptown and Peacehaven.
item.content:encoded:
Chris Ward MP for Brighton Kemptown and Peacehaven has welcomed £8.1 million in funding for East Sussex County Council, as part of the Labour government’s plan to fix the SEND system and end the postcode lottery in support for children and young people.

The investment, part of more than £3 billion to create inclusive classrooms across the country, will help ensure more local children can achieve and thrive at their local school, with improved facilities and earlier access to specialist support.

Around £860 million will be allocated to every council in England – the biggest ever single high needs capital allocation – to create more inclusive physical spaces and drive a transformative expansion of Inclusion Bases, so that early specialist support becomes part of the fabric of mainstream schools. East Sussex County Council is set to receive £8,133,212.29, which is £1.1 million extra compared to last year.

To make sure all children and families in Brighton Kemptown and Peacehaven can benefit from truly inclusive schools, East Sussex will be required to sign a written agreement to prioritise increasing places in mainstream schools.

Signing the agreement, East Sussex will need to adopt inclusive strategies that support positive outcomes for children, like reducing the number of children with SEND who need to travel long distances to school, and making sure every child who needs a place in an Inclusion Base can access one. 

Importantly, councils should also use their allocation to ensure sufficient special school and AP places for children and young people with the most complex needs, reflecting the vital role these settings play in providing support for some of the most vulnerable children.

Where councils choose to spend their funding on special school places, they should set out clear evidence-based rationale for why this would better respond to local need – ensuring investment in places delivers for local families.

This announcement follows the government’s landmark SEND reforms and is a significant step towards ending the postcode lottery of SEND support, so every child has access to a brilliant, inclusive education closer to home in Brighton Kemptown and Peacehaven.

Schools Minister, Georgia Gould said:

“Just last month, we set out once-in-a-generation SEND reforms and we’re now acting quickly to deliver the changes that make sure more children can thrive in their local community, with their friends.

“Inclusion is a choice, one this government is proud to make, and the funding we’re backing councils with will help make it a reality.

“Our SEND reforms will deliver specialist places where they are needed and ensure every secondary school has a space designed for children with SEND which they – and their families – can rely on. This is a further step towards rebuilding trust in the system and transforming outcomes for young people.”

Commenting, Chris Ward MP said:

“For far too long, Governments have been neglected issues surrounding SEND. That’s why it has been a huge priority for this Labour Government to introduce a new SEND reform package.

“I’ve ensured the voices of local families across my constituency have been heard throughout the process. I hosted a SEND summit in Saltdean last year, which brought together parents, teachers, support staff and specialists to discuss the challenges they face, as well as improvements they would like to see.

“This Labour government is investing to make these changes a reality, providing East Sussex County Council with £8,133,212.29 to create specialist places and training up teachers to meet a range of needs.

“Brick by brick, Labour is building an education system where every child in Brighton Kemptown and Peacehaven can achieve and thrive.”

The £8,133,212.29 comes alongside £500 million per year allocated to nurseries, schools and colleges to help them deliver an improved inclusion offer. New estimates published this week suggest that the average primary school will receive around £14,000 through the Inclusive Mainstream Fund, on top of their core funding allocations, with the average secondary school receiving around £48,000 in 2026-27. 

Inclusion Bases enable pupils to move seamlessly between specialist support and mainstream classes, meeting a wide range of needs and importantly, building confidence and belonging. 

East Sussex’s high needs capital cash boost can also be used to make the school environment inclusive by design through adaptations to improve inclusivity and accessibility, like providing a more comfortable sensory environment by enhancing ventilation, acoustics and lighting.

Following the publication of the schools white paper and Education Estates Strategy, the funding allocated this week marks an important step to realising an inclusive education that delivers high standards for all pupils in every setting.

The post Chris Ward MP champions biggest single funding allocation to deliver more places for children with SEND appeared first on Chris Ward | MP for Brighton Kemptown and Peacehaven.

item.title: Chris Ward MP sees Government and Council investment in action at Lewes Road Bus Depot
item.dc:creator: Chris Ward
item.description:
Chris Ward MP for Brighton Kemptown and Peacehaven visited the Lewes Road bus depot, where he saw first-hand how Government and Council investment is supporting the transition to greener public transport in the city. During the visit, Chris toured the depot and met with staff responsible for keeping Brighton & Hove moving. He observed how...

The post Chris Ward MP sees Government and Council investment in action at Lewes Road Bus Depot appeared first on Chris Ward | MP for Brighton Kemptown and Peacehaven.
item.content:encoded:
Chris Ward MP for Brighton Kemptown and Peacehaven visited the Lewes Road bus depot, where he saw first-hand how Government and Council investment is supporting the transition to greener public transport in the city.

During the visit, Chris toured the depot and met with staff responsible for keeping Brighton & Hove moving. He observed how buses are maintained, saw seats being manufactured, and stepped aboard one of the new buses now entering service.

Chris also heard about the new electric fleet, made possible through funding from the Department for Transport, which awarded an initial £2.9 million to support a £10.5 million partnership between Brighton & Hove City Council and Brighton & Hove Buses. This funding is enabling the introduction of 16 electric buses, alongside the necessary charging infrastructure.

A further £2.3 million has since been secured through the same scheme, allowing an additional 13 electric buses to be rolled out across the network by September 2026.

Following the tour, Chris met with representatives from Brighton & Hove Buses to discuss their priorities and future plans for local services. He also took the opportunity to raise a number of concerns that residents have shared with him in relation to bus routes and reliability.

Commenting, Chris Ward MP said:

“I was pleased to visit Lewes Road bus depot and see the incredible work that’s going on to keep our city moving.

“I’ve been making the case for investment in our local bus network, so I’m really pleased the Government has backed Brighton with this funding to deliver new electric buses and better infrastructure.

“Brighton has the highest bus usage outside of London, and they’re a lifeline for many people across my constituency. So it was important to raise the concerns residents have shared with me around bus routes and reliability. We discussed proposed changes to bus routes across Peacehaven, as well as potential solutions to improve the reliability of buses around Bevendean and Woodingdean.”

Commenting, Rupert Cox Interim Managing Director, Brighton & Hove Buses and Metrobus, said:

“Welcoming Chris to our Lewes Road depot was a great opportunity to showcase the hard work our teams do every day to keep Brighton & Hove moving. The funding secured through the Department for Transport and the City Council is helping us grow our zero‑emissions fleet and providing cleaner air for our communities. We’ll continue working with partners and local residents to improve reliability and make our network even better.”

The post Chris Ward MP sees Government and Council investment in action at Lewes Road Bus Depot appeared first on Chris Ward | MP for Brighton Kemptown and Peacehaven.

item.title: Chris Ward MP takes part in Student Q&A at Coombe Road Primary School
item.dc:creator: Chris Ward
item.description:
On Friday 27 February, Chris Ward MP for Brighton Kemptown and Peacehaven joined Year 6 pupils at Coombe Road Primary School for a lively Q&A session. Over 45 minutes, students quizzed the MP on a wide range of topics including climate change, immigration and policing. They were also keen to understand what being an MP...

The post Chris Ward MP takes part in Student Q&A at Coombe Road Primary School appeared first on Chris Ward | MP for Brighton Kemptown and Peacehaven.
item.content:encoded:
On Friday 27 February, Chris Ward MP for Brighton Kemptown and Peacehaven joined Year 6 pupils at Coombe Road Primary School for a lively Q&A session.

Over 45 minutes, students quizzed the MP on a wide range of topics including climate change, immigration and policing. They were also keen to understand what being an MP involves. Mr Ward explained that his role is “essentially three roles”, balancing his ministerial responsibilities with his work in Westminster and across Brighton Kemptown and Peacehaven.

He spoke about the importance of politics and encouraged the students to get involved early, whether by campaigning on issues they care about or engaging in their local community.

Commenting, Chris Ward MP said:

“It was a pleasure to speak to pupils at Coombe Road Primary School, who were incredibly welcoming and engaged.

“Their questions were thoughtful and insightful, and it was great to see such enthusiasm for learning more about how politics works. I encouraged them all to get involved and speak up on the issues that matter to them.”

Commenting, Mr Scrase, the headteacher said:

“Mr Ward is always welcome at Coombe Road and the children loved quizzing him on topics ranging from immigration to social media. They thoroughly enjoyed hearing about his work in Parliament and how democracy functions in everyday life.”

The children spoke confidently about their own democratic experiences through School Council meetings, Class Charters, and the House system. They showed a clear understanding of the school’s  values – respect, kindness, responsibility, and curiosity – and how these connect to wider British Values.”

Commenting, a child in year 6 said:

‘We might not get a 3-day weekend but I thought he was very professional!”

The post Chris Ward MP takes part in Student Q&A at Coombe Road Primary School appeared first on Chris Ward | MP for Brighton Kemptown and Peacehaven.

item.title: Chris Ward MP hosts Constituent’s Coffee Morning
item.dc:creator: Chris Ward
item.description:
On Friday 27 February, Chris Ward MP for Brighton Kemptown and Peacehaven hosted a well-attended Coffee Morning for residents at the Brighton & Hove Albion Football Club (BHAFC) Foundation Community Hub in the heart of Queen’s Park. The event brought together constituents from across the area for an open and informal discussion about the issues...

The post Chris Ward MP hosts Constituent’s Coffee Morning appeared first on Chris Ward | MP for Brighton Kemptown and Peacehaven.
item.content:encoded:
On Friday 27 February, Chris Ward MP for Brighton Kemptown and Peacehaven hosted a well-attended Coffee Morning for residents at the Brighton & Hove Albion Football Club (BHAFC) Foundation Community Hub in the heart of Queen’s Park.

The event brought together constituents from across the area for an open and informal discussion about the issues that matter most to them. Over tea and coffee, residents raised a wide range of topics, reflecting both national debates and local concerns. Conversations covered calls to extend paternity leave, ongoing challenges around social housing, and parking pressures across Brighton and Hove, as well as individual casework affecting families in the constituency.

Coffee mornings are one of the ways Members of Parliament stay directly connected to the communities they represent. They provide an accessible setting for residents to share their experiences, highlight concerns and ask questions about both Government policy and local issues. For many, it is also an opportunity to better understand the role of their MP and how their office can offer support.

Mr Ward has previously hosted a coffee morning at Saltdean Lido and plans to continue holding similar sessions in different locations across Brighton Kemptown and Peacehaven. He hopes to make events as accessible as possible for residents as he moves the location around the constituency.

Mr Ward is encouraging any constituents who would like to attend a future Coffee Morning to keep an eye on his social media pages (@ChrisWardMP) for details of upcoming dates and venues. Alternatively, residents can email chris.ward.mp@parliament.uk to request to be added to his coffee morning waiting list. Please provide your full residential address, as well as details on the issues you would like to raise. 

Community groups, local venues and organisations interested in hosting a future session are also warmly invited to contact his office to discuss arrangements.

Commenting, Chris Ward MP said:

“It was a pleasure to meet with residents at the BHAFC Foundation Community Hub in Queen’s Park, and I’m very grateful to everyone who took the time out of their day to come along.

“I always value the opportunity to speak with constituents in an open and informal setting like this. It’s so important for me to understand what’s affecting people here in Queen’s Park and across the wider constituency, and to hear directly how I can support them as their MP.

“I’m also hugely thankful to the Community Hub for hosting us in such brilliant Albion-themed surroundings.

“If you’re interested in attending my next coffee morning, please email my office chris.ward.mp@parliament.uk with your full residential address and a brief outline of what you’d like to raise with me. We’ll be in touch with information on our next event!”

The post Chris Ward MP hosts Constituent’s Coffee Morning appeared first on Chris Ward | MP for Brighton Kemptown and Peacehaven.

item.title: Queen’s Park Coffee Morning
item.dc:creator: Chris Ward
item.description:
I’m hosting a Coffee Morning! These events are an opportunity for me to update you on my work as your MP, as well as a chance to chat to me about the issues that are important to you. Register using the ‘contact’ form in the top right hand corner. Enter your full address details and...

The post Queen’s Park Coffee Morning appeared first on Chris Ward | MP for Brighton Kemptown and Peacehaven.
item.content:encoded:
I’m hosting a Coffee Morning!

These events are an opportunity for me to update you on my work as your MP, as well as a chance to chat to me about the issues that are important to you.

Register using the ‘contact’ form in the top right hand corner. Enter your full address details and provide a brief outline of what you’d like to discuss.

Please note that for security reasons, the exact location will be shared with you 24 hours before the event. Spaces for the venue are limited, so register soon to secure your place.

The post Queen’s Park Coffee Morning appeared first on Chris Ward | MP for Brighton Kemptown and Peacehaven.

item.title: Brighton General Hospital site must stay in public hands, MP warns
item.dc:creator: Chris Ward
item.description:
Chris Ward MP has reaffirmed his commitment to unlocking the long-stalled Brighton General Hospital site for public benefit, following a meeting last night with NHS leaders, community organisations, students and teachers. The meeting brought together representatives from Sussex Community NHS Foundation Trust, Brighton & Hove Citizens, and local education leaders to discuss the future of...

The post Brighton General Hospital site must stay in public hands, MP warns appeared first on Chris Ward | MP for Brighton Kemptown and Peacehaven.
item.content:encoded:
Chris Ward MP has reaffirmed his commitment to unlocking the long-stalled Brighton General Hospital site for public benefit, following a meeting last night with NHS leaders, community organisations, students and teachers.

The meeting brought together representatives from Sussex Community NHS Foundation Trust, Brighton & Hove Citizens, and local education leaders to discuss the future of the Brighton General Hospital site and how it can better serve the city.

Chris Ward highlighted the site’s unique potential to address two of Brighton’s most pressing challenges: the severe shortage of affordable and key-worker housing, and the need for modern, community-based NHS facilities.

Praising the dedication of staff working at the hospital, Chris noted that the site’s ageing infrastructure is no longer fit for the future and that progress has been stalled for too many years.

Chris Ward MP said:

“Brighton General Hospital must remain in public hands, serving the people of this City.

This site has the potential to help solve two major problems: the chronic shortage of affordable homes in Brighton and the pressure on our NHS.

“I’m working closely with the council, community partners and government to move this forward after years of drift. This land was historically gifted to the people of Brighton, and it should remain in public hands, delivering affordable homes for key workers and improving NHS care for local people.

“There is real momentum building now, and I will continue to push hard to make sure we secure the right outcome for the community.”

Chris Ward will continue engaging with NHS partners, community groups and residents to ensure that future proposals reflect local needs and keep the site in public ownership.

The post Brighton General Hospital site must stay in public hands, MP warns appeared first on Chris Ward | MP for Brighton Kemptown and Peacehaven.

item.title: Chris Ward MP welcomes latest Morrisons update
item.dc:creator: Chris Ward
item.description:
Chris Ward, MP for Brighton Kemptown and Peacehaven, has welcomed confirmation from Morrisons that it remains on track to submit a revised planning application for the new supermarket at the Meridian Centre in March. Morrisons has indicated that it hopes to bring the revised application before Lewes District Council’s Planning Committee in June, marking a...

The post Chris Ward MP welcomes latest Morrisons update appeared first on Chris Ward | MP for Brighton Kemptown and Peacehaven.
item.content:encoded:
Chris Ward, MP for Brighton Kemptown and Peacehaven, has welcomed confirmation from Morrisons that it remains on track to submit a revised planning application for the new supermarket at the Meridian Centre in March.

Morrisons has indicated that it hopes to bring the revised application before Lewes District Council’s Planning Committee in June, marking a significant step forward in the long-awaited redevelopment of the site.

Chris Ward has consistently pressed Morrisons to honour its commitments to Peacehaven residents after a series of unacceptable delays, acknowledging that it is the single biggest issue in the Peacehaven and Telscombe area. He previously started a petition to urge Morrisons to build its new supermarket, which received over 2000 signatures, and attended a protest in September urging Morrisons to “build it or sell it”.

The MP confirmed he will continue working with Morrisons, local councillors and community representatives to ensure the revised application proceeds according to the proposed timetable and that residents are kept informed at every stage.

Commenting, Chris Ward MP said:

“After so many unacceptable delays, I am relieved and optimistic that Morrisons remain committed to the timetable they previously promised. It is positive that the communication from Morrisons has improved and that they have reiterated their commitment to bring this forward.

“I have been speaking to Morrisons regularly over the last 18 months and doing everything I can to ensure the voices of Peaceahaven residents are heard loud and clear.

“This progress would not have been possible without the strength of the community-led campaign. Peacehaven has been very clear that it expects delivery, and I will continue to do all I can to make sure Morrisons delivers.

“I’m delighted with what we have achieved so far, but there is still a lot more to do. What matters now is turning commitments into concrete action and finally delivering the supermarket and regeneration that Peacehaven deserves.”

The post Chris Ward MP welcomes latest Morrisons update appeared first on Chris Ward | MP for Brighton Kemptown and Peacehaven.
